page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs derived from 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, utilizing Intel Xeon 6455B processors running at a high clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor architecture includes a 60 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix multiplication workloads.

The instance is equipped with 1024.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local NVMe SSD storage, meaning all storage must be network-attached. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps, facilitating rapid data transfers for distributed environments.

In benchmark testing, the r7iz.32xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores rank in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth for large block reads (474.63 GB/sec) and writes (213.9 GB/sec) also reaches the top 10%, though the Passmark memory score falls in the middle 50%. The hardware shows strong capabilities in database operations, with Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations (149.32 million operations per second) both scoring in the top 10%. Additionally, the AMX instructions facilitate top-tier LLM inference speeds for medium-sized (7B) models.

Given its high-density DDR5 memory and high-frequency Intel Xeon processors, this instance offers a specialized hardware profile that trades local storage for massive memory capacity and compute throughput. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high memory-to-vCPU ratios alongside high-frequency single-core and multi-core execution. Appropriate workloads include large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and CPU-based machine learning or LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs via Intel Xeon 6455B processors running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 1024.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz. The instance lacks local storage but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark results place this server in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, large-block memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET operations reaching 149.32 million operations per second. Supported by Intel AVX-512 and AMX extensions, it delivers top-tier performance for medium-sized LLM inference. The r7iz.32xlarge is designed for memory-intensive and compute-heavy enterprise workloads, including large-scale databases, in-memory caches, and data-intensive applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier database and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise databases and memory-intensive computing.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6455B processors.
1024.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (5 GHz) for memory-intensive tasks.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support accelerating machine learning and cryptographic workloads.
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with network-attached storage configuration.
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and CPU-based LLM inference.
